compiled_state.title: ticket CLI: -p is silently ignored in ticket create -> agents expect it as --parent shorthand and must recreate tickets
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed
`ticket create task <title> -p <parent-id>` silently ignores the `-p` flag and creates the ticket with `parent_id: null`. The ticket creation succeeds with no error or warning.

## Expected
Either (a) `-p` is accepted as an alias for `--parent`, or (b) the CLI errors with 'unknown flag: -p' so the agent knows to use `--parent`.

## Impact
During epic 0b6c-08ba sprint (2026-04-12), T2 and T3 tasks were created 3 times each due to this silent failure. Each recreation required closing the previous ticket and recreating with the correct `--parent` flag.

## Reproduction
`.claude/scripts/dso ticket create task 'test title' -p <any-id>` — ticket is created with parent_id=null, no error.

## Fix Options
1. Add `-p` as alias for `--parent` in ticket create
2. Reject unrecognized short flags with a clear error message
